label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem. So Jane was born on the last day of February in 2000. Today is her 16th birthday. We need to find the date one week from today in MM/DD/YYYY format. The options are given, so maybe we can check each one. First, the last day of February is usually the 29th, right? But wait, February 2000... Let me think. 2000 is a leap year? Hmm, leap years are divisible by 4, but if they're divisible by 100, they must also be divisible by 400. So 2000 divided by 400 is 5, so yes, 2000 was a leap year. So Jane was born on 2/29/2000. Now, today is her 16th birthday. So 16 years after 2000 is 2016. But wait, does that mean today is 2/29/2016? But 2016 is also a leap
